sanskrit:
बुद्ध्या विशुद्धया युक्तो धृत्या आत्मानं नियम्य च।
शब्दादीन् विषयांस्त्यक्त्वा रागद्वेषौ व्युदस्य च।।
विविक्तसेवी लघ्वाशी यतवाक्कायमानसः।
ध्यानयोगपरो नित्यं वैराग्यं समुपाश्रितः।।
अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं परिग्रहम्।
विमुच्य निर्ममः शान्तो ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते।।
transliteration: buddhyā viśuddhayā yukto dhṛtyātmānaṃ niyamya ca | śabdādīn viṣayāṃs tyaktvā rāga-dveṣau vyudasya ca || vivikta-sevī laghv-āśī yata-vāk-kāya-mānasaḥ | dhyāna-yoga-paro nityaṃ vairāgyaṃ samupāśritaḥ || ahaṅkāraṃ balaṃ darpaṃ kāmaṃ krodhaṃ parigraham | vimucya nirmamaḥ śānto brahma-bhūyāya kalpate ||
literal_meaning: With intelligence fully purified, yoked by determination, having restrained the self, having abandoned sense-objects such as sound, having cast aside attachment and aversion; dwelling in solitude, eating lightly, controlling speech, body and mind, constantly devoted to the yoga of meditation, having taken refuge in dispassion; freed from ego, false strength, arrogance, desire, anger and possessiveness, without ‘mine-ness’, peaceful—one becomes fit for Brahman-realization.
contextual_meaning: Krishna gives Arjuna a precise protocol for ego-dissolution: first sanitize perception (viśuddha-buddhi), then systematically de-condition every sensory, emotional and cognitive hook that keeps the self tethered to the object-world. The result is not repression but a tranquil ‘zero-point’ from which action arises without residue.
psychological_state: Post-deconditioning equanimity: the nervous system is no longer high-jacked by reward/aversion loops; identity shifts from personality to awareness itself.
trigger_scenario: A high-performing professional deletes social-media apps, moves to a quiet cabin, eats two simple meals a day, and notices the mind’s frantic FOMO stories quieting down—realizing that worth is not tied to visibility.
anchor_text: Empty the mind of noise, then act from the quiet that remains.
